# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that creates releases and publishes packages. On version tags, build the application, create a GitHub release, and publish to npm/PyPI. Make the release process fully automated so developers can just push tags to trigger releases.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI / CD Release Workflow
on: 
    tag_push:
        types: [created] # This event is activated when a new git tag gets created (i.e., pushed with 'git push')    
jobs:  
# Run the build job to create releases and publish packages on version tags     
  release-job:      
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest        
    steps:        # These are what's run in each step of our workflow          
                     - uses: actions/checkout@v2                # Retrieve the package source code from your repository         
                  
                     - name: Setup Node.js environment      
                       uses: actions/setup-node@v1               # This action sets up a specific version of node     
                       with:          
                           node-version: 14                        # You may change this to an older version        
                   
                  
                     - name: Install dependencies           
                       run: npm ci                      # Use 'npm install' here         
                         
                 
                     - name: Build Application    
                       env :                // This should contain all your credentials as environment variables  
                         GITHUB_TOKEN       // The token for authenticating with GitHub     
                           CI                 // To trigger actions in the context of this workflow        
                      run: npm run build  # You can replace 'npm run build' to use a different command                .    (e.g., `ng build` or other)  
                   
                     - name : Publish Application             
                       env :                   // Similarly, you will set up your environment here         
                         NPM_TOKEN             # The token for authenticating with npm       
                           PACKAGE_NAME           # Name of the package (usually generated by `npm pack`)      
                          run: npm publish    .   # Use 'npm login' first if not logged in already          (# replace '.')      , to trigger actions on this workflow            
                    - name : Create Release                
                      id: create_release           // Unique ID for the release        
                       uses: my-own/action@0.15.2  # GitHub's recommended way of creating a new releas     e          (you will need to replace `my-own` and version with your actual actions)  
                     parameters:                  // This is where you define the release note, asset path      , etc        (. See https://github.com/actions/create-release#publishing-assets for more details )       .    (e.g., -f bundle; url="https:/example...')               run : echo "${GITHUB_RELEASE}"               
                    # Publish release notes        (. See https://github.com/actions/create-release#publishing-assets for more details )         .   (e.g., -F bundle; url="https:/example... ")  run : echo "${GITHUB_RELEASE}"
